item.title: AI's Economics Don't Make Sense [Ad Free] 摘要
item.description:
GitHub Copilot is moving to usage-based billing on June 1, 2026, ending the flat-rate subscription model. Microsoft cites unsustainable inference costs from agentic usage, where a single request can cost $11 in tokens. This shift reflects a broader industry trend: AI services have been subsidized by venture capital, with users often consuming far more compute than their subscription fee covers. The change has sparked user backlash, with many declaring the product 'dead.' The article argues that the subscription model is fundamentally broken for LLM-based services, as token costs remain high and reasoning models increase consumption.
标签: GitHub Copilot, pricing, AI economics, subscription model, usage-based billing, Microsoft, inference costs, developer tools, SaaS

item.title: AI's Economics Don't Make Sense 摘要
item.description:
GitHub Copilot is transitioning to usage-based billing on June 1, 2026, replacing the current request-based model. Microsoft cites rising inference costs from agentic AI usage as unsustainable. Users will receive token credits equal to their subscription fee (e.g., $19 worth of tokens for a $19 plan). This shift reflects a broader industry trend where AI services, previously subsidized, are raising prices to match actual compute costs. The article argues that the subscription model is fundamentally broken for LLM-based services, comparing it to Uber charging for gas separately. Users are reacting negatively, with many declaring the product 'dead.'
标签: GitHub Copilot, usage-based pricing, AI economics, Microsoft, subscription model, inference costs, subprime AI crisis, developer tools, SaaS pricing, generative AI

item.title: The 3rd Annual Blog Post Competition, Extravaganza, and Jamboree 摘要
item.description:
The author announces the third annual Experimental History Blog Competition, inviting submissions of unpublished blog posts for cash prizes ($500, $250, $100). The post provides inspiration by categorizing exemplary blog posts from various genres: book reviews, deep dives, big ideas, personal stories, scientific reports, how-to guides, and more. Submission instructions require pasting the post into a Google Doc with public sharing settings and submitting via a Google Form by June 15. Previous winners are ineligible, but honorable mentions may enter again. The competition aims to encourage writing and showcase diverse voices.
标签: blogging, writing competition, substack, essays, creative writing, online community, prizes, call for submissions, personal stories, science writing

item.title: 10Gb Ethernet: what I had to (re)learn 摘要
item.description:
The author upgrades home networking to 10Gb after their ISP offered a 10Gb connection. They trace Ethernet history from 10BASE2 coaxial daisy-chains to 10BASE-T hubs, then to modern switches. They explain the evolution of SFP/SFP+ modules for backbone connections and fibre optics. A key issue is that 10GBASE-T over RJ45 runs hot and requires CAT-6A cabling, while older CAT-5E may not support it reliably. The interim 2.5Gb/s and 5Gb/s standards were created to work on existing infrastructure. The author hints at using SFP+ with DAC or fibre to avoid thermal and cabling problems, with a follow-up post detailing their actual setup.
标签: networking, Ethernet, 10GbE, home networking, SFP, cabling, ISP, history, hardware, upgrade

item.title: Turning a trick into a technique 摘要
item.description:
The article discusses turning a mathematical trick into a general technique for approximating even functions. By subtracting a multiple of one even function from another, higher-order approximations can be created by canceling lower-order terms. As an example, the Bessel function J0(x) is approximated using cosine: J0(x) ≈ (1 + cos(x))/2, which cancels the x^2 term and yields a fourth-order accurate approximation. Numerical tests show excellent agreement for small x, demonstrating the technique's effectiveness.
标签: mathematics, approximation, even functions, Bessel functions, cosine, series expansion, numerical methods, trick, technique

item.title: Quoting OpenAI Codex base_instructions 摘要
item.description:
OpenAI's Codex base instructions for GPT-5.5 include a rule to avoid mentioning goblins, gremlins, raccoons, trolls, ogres, pigeons, or other animals/creatures unless absolutely relevant to the user's query. This is part of a set of system prompts designed to guide the model's behavior and ensure focused, appropriate responses.
标签: openai, ai, llms, system-prompts, prompt-engineering, codex-cli, generative-ai, gpt, guidelines, safety

item.title: OpenAI Projects ChatGPT Plus subscriptions to drop by 80% from 44 Million in 2025 to 9 Million In 2026, Made Up Using Cheaper Subscriptions (Somehow) 摘要
item.description:
OpenAI projects an 80% decline in ChatGPT Plus subscribers from 44 million in 2025 to 9 million in 2026, while expecting its cheaper, ad-supported ChatGPT Go subscriptions to surge from 3 million to 112 million in the same period. This would require a 3600% year-over-year increase. Even at $5/month, the revenue would fall $155 million short of current Plus revenue. The projections suggest OpenAI anticipates a major shift toward lower-priced, ad-supported tiers, potentially increasing its burn rate significantly.
标签: OpenAI, ChatGPT, subscriptions, revenue, ad-supported, AI, business model, projections, user growth, burn rate
